old_exact: [¶13] To establish the second prong, "the defendant must specify how and where trial counsel was incompetent and the probable different result." Brewer, 2019 ND 69, ¶ 9 (quoting Middleton v. State, 2014 ND 144, ¶ 6, 849 N.W.2d 196). "A reasonable probability is a probability sufficient to undermine confidence in the outcome." Brewer, at ¶ 9 (quoting Middleton, at ¶ 6; Strickland, 466 U.S. at 694). "If it is easier to dispose of an ineffectiveness claim on the ground of lack of sufficient prejudice, which we expect will often be so, that course should be followed." Rourke, 2018 ND 137, ¶ 6. A
